gaps: Personal Data definition narrowly limited to raw uploaded records while excluding transformed outputs, statistical summaries, service measurements, and derived data, leaving normalization service results unprotected.
No specified security controls (encryption at rest/transit, access restrictions, logging, vulnerability management, incident response); only vague 'commercially suitable' safeguards mentioned.
Personnel confidentiality obligations not mandated—only internal onboarding materials referenced, no binding confidentiality duty for staff accessing Personal Data.
